Best view of the central Manhattan area as it is exactly between the Empire State Building and Central Park, although it is not the highest viewpoint.
In addition, it seemed to me less competitive than other places and with a good store, plus a better bar than the like.
Deserves a conferred

We went on a Thursday afternoon in May at 2 p.m. Almost no line so we were whisked up to the observation deck quick. It was a clear day so great view in all directions. It was expensive, but all the observation decks in NYC are expensive. It comes with the territory.

What is especially nice is that the top floor of the 3-story observation deck is open, do you can take photos with no glass wall in the way. Very nice. All in all, worth the price.

Incredibly beautiful 360° view of NYC. We went up in May around 6:30 PM and stayed until after sunset, so we could admire the city during the day, at dusk, and in the dark. It got very crowded at sunset. For us, one of the best views of the city overall. 
Only the disorganized and long line when going down slightly marred the experience (we needed almost an hour to get to the elevator and initially did not know if we were in the right line due to a lack of staff).
